entities.name: Francis Geisdorff
entities.context: Named in patent title notation as 'Francis Geisdorfe -patent-Illegal, 1919'; likely same as Frederick/Francis Geisdorff

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
entities.context: Crow allottee #2336, deceased November 27, 1918, aged about 20, born May 6, 1898, lived at Randlett/Fort Duchesne, Utah

entities.name: Frederick Geisdorff Sr.
entities.context: Father of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; received papers pertaining to leasing from Superintendent Asbury

entities.name: Emily J. Geisdorff
entities.context: Widow of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; lived at Fort Duchesne, Utah; also referenced as Emily M. Geisdorff and Emily F. Geisdorff

entities.name: Emily Lucile Geisdorff
entities.context: Minor daughter of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff; born May 23, 1918

entities.name: Thomas R. Powers
entities.context: Purchaser of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment; of Wyola, Montana and Hartman, Wyoming; bid $1,500 for 159.67 acres

entities.name: C. H. Asbury
entities.context: Superintendent, Crow Agency, Montana; also referenced as Superintendent of U.S. Indian School

entities.name: Albert H. Kneale
entities.context: Superintendent, Uintah and Ouray Agency, Fort Duchesne, Utah; assisted with sale matters

entities.name: Calvin Coolidge
entities.context: President of the United States; signed trust patent for Francis/Francis Geisdorff dated December 21, 1923

entities.name: Woodrow Wilson
entities.context: President of the United States; signed fee patent No. 719146 dated November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: John McPhaul
entities.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner, General Land Office; signed statement October 13, 1919 that no reservations or withdrawals covered the land

entities.name: Crow Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency in Montana managing Crow Reservation allotments

entities.name: Uintah and Ouray Agency
entities.context: U.S. Indian Service agency at Fort Duchesne, Utah; jurisdiction over Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow

entities.name: Office of Indian Affairs
entities.context: Federal bureau overseeing Indian land sales and allotments; Washington D.C.

entities.name: General Land Office
entities.context: Federal office issuing patents; located in Washington D.C.; issued fee patent No. 719146

entities.name: Department of the Interior
entities.context: Federal department overseeing Indian Affairs and land patents

entities.name: Crow Tribe of Indians
entities.context: Tribal entity; argued to be rightful owner of land upon allottee's death per trust patent terms

entities.name: Merchants National Bank of Billings
entities.context: Bank in Billings, Montana holding $7.98 lease money deposited to credit of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s estate

entities.name: Sheridan Banking Company
entities.context: Bank on which T.R. Powers drew checks dated November 2, 1918 for $1,350 and $150

entities.name: Crow Reservation
entities.context: Montana reservation where Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was located

entities.name: Fort Duchesne, Utah
entities.context: Location of Uintah and Ouray Agency; residence of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and his widow

entities.name: Randlett, Utah
entities.context: Residence of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. at time of allotment sale proceedings

entities.name: Wyola, Montana
entities.context: Address of Thomas R. Powers

entities.name: Hartman, Wyoming
entities.context: Address of Thomas R. Powers as of December 1918

entities.name: Washington, D.C.
entities.context: Location of Office of Indian Affairs and General Land Office

entities.name: Billings, Montana
entities.context: Location of U.S. Land Office and Merchants National Bank

entities.context: Location of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment No. 2336; S½NE¼, SE¼NW¼, and Lot 2 (39.67 acres), 159.67 acres total

entities.context: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s Crow allotment; 159.67 acres in Section 19, T.9S, R.34E, Montana Meridian

entities.name: Crow Land Sale No. 991
entities.context: Official sale number for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment sold to Thomas R. Powers

entities.context: Location of Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment; Lots 9, 10, 11, 12 of Section 2 and various quarter sections of Section 11; 648.04 acres

entities.context: Trust patent number for Francis/Eloise Whitebear Pease collection allotment dated December 21, 1923

entities.name: Fee Patent No. 719146
entities.context: Fee patent issued November 14, 1919 to Thomas R. Powers for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment

entities.context: Act under which Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment was made

entities.name: Act of April 11, 1882 (22 Stat. L. 42)
entities.context: Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was made

entities.name: Act of February 8, 1887 (24 Stat. L. 388)
entities.context: Dawes Act; trust patent for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment issued under this act on December 2, 1907

entities.name: Act of May 18, 1916 (39 Stat. 123)
entities.context: Referenced in fee patent approval for Thomas R. Powers regarding water rights

entities.name: Eloise Whitebear Pease
entities.context: Named on collection stamps throughout document; collector/owner of these historical documents

entities.name: 159.67 acres (Allotment 2336)
entities.context: Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment; S½NE¼, SE¼NW¼, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S, R.34E

entities.name: 648.04 acres (Francis Geisdorff allotment)
entities.context: Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment in Section 2 and Section 11, T.1S, R.34E

events.description: Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotted 159.67 acres (S½NE¼, SE¼NW¼, Lot 2, Section 19, T.9S, R.34E) as Crow Allottee #2336; trust patent issued December 2, 1907 under Act of February 8, 1887

Francis Geisdorff allotted 648.04 acres as homestead (Lots 9-12, Section 2 and portions of Section 11, T.1S, R.34E) under Act of June 4, 1920; trust patent No. 227584 signed by President Calvin Coolidge
Petition for Sale of Land by original allottee Frederick Geisdorff Jr. received at Crow Agency
Thomas R. Powers made an offer to lease Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment and adjacent land owned by relatives
Appraisal made pertaining to Powers' request to lease the land; government farmer appraised land at $1,194
Crow Superintendent C.H. Asbury wrote to Frederick Geisdorff Sr. sending papers pertaining to leasing and requesting answer
Crow Agency offered for sale the allotment of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. on petition acknowledged before Supt. Kneale of Uintah and Ouray Agency
Acceptance of sale of allotment sent to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. for his signature
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. signed and returned acceptance form to Crow office
Thomas R. Powers notified that owners of land under Crow Land Sale No. 991 accepted his bid of $1,500 for 159.67 acres; remittance of balance demanded
Superintendent acknowledged receipt of two checks from T.R. Powers for $1,350 and $150 covering balance of purchase price and advertising fee
Letter written to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. noting he was only 20 years of age and doubting Indian Bureau would approve sale; copy sent to Supt. Kneale
Frederick Geisdorff Jr. died of influenza, aged about 20, married, intestate, survived by wife Emily J. Geisdorff and daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Superintendent Kneale wrote short letter to Crow office stating 'Fritz died a few days ago of influenza'
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Thomas R. Powers regarding age issue of allottee and offering to cancel sale and return payment
Emily Lucile Geisdorff born to Frederick Geisdorff Jr. and Emily J. Geisdorff
Uintah and Ouray Agency notified Supt. Asbury of birth of Emily Lucile Geisdorff, born May 23, 1918
Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff wrote to Crow Agency Superintendent inquiring if agency could aid her in paying note owed by her deceased husband
Superintendent answered Mrs. Geisdorff's letter regretting no funds available; noted sale pending but uncertain due to allottee's age at time of advertisement
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Commissioner of Indian Affairs reviewing all matters concerning land sale; holding checks from Powers for $1,350 and $150
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily M. Geisdorff enclosing copy of letter to Commissioner and asking if she wanted pending sale completed

Office of Indian Affairs responded to Asbury's March 24 letter; stated nothing to show brother, sister, or widow want sale to go through; if heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers
Superintendent Asbury wrote to Mrs. Emily F. Geisdorff asking if she wished sale completed to Powers for $1,500 or bid rejected; enclosed authority to put old sale through
Superintendent Asbury enclosed sale papers to Commissioner stating he had just received acceptance of widow of allottee
Hearing held to determine heirs of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.; testimony taken by Supt. Kneale at Fort Duchesne
General Land Office stated no reservations or withdrawals covering land and no reasons why fee patent should not be issued; signed by John McPhaul, Acting Assistant Commissioner
Fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers for 159.67 acres of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment; signed by President Woodrow Wilson
Office of Indian Affairs transmitted fee patent No. 719146 to Crow Agency Superintendent for delivery to Thomas R. Powers upon signing receipt
Superintendent notified U.S. Land Office Billings of fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336 of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.

Superintendent wrote to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff informing her that after heirs determined, proceeds of sale could be transferred to her agency upon request; minor's shares cannot be paid without special authority
Report on heirship and recommendations transmitted to Secretary of Interior regarding estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr., deceased Crow allottee No. 2336

Check No. 10613 for $1,317.26 sent to Mrs. Emily J. Geisdorff representing her share (one half plus dower interest) of estate proceeds; $188.90 credited to daughter Emily; $15.00 deducted for heirship fee from total of $1,521.24
Report prepared analyzing the Geisdorff land sale as illegal; argued land should have reverted to Crow Tribe upon allottee's death per trust patent terms and Treaty of 1968

financial_transactions.payee: Estate of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. / Crow Agency
financial_transactions.description: Purchase of 159.67 acres (Allotment No. 2336) under Crow Land Sale No. 991; paid as $150 deposit with bid and $1,350 balance check dated November 2, 1918 on Sheridan Banking Company
Advertising fee for Crow Land Sale No. 991 (purchase price over $1,000 and under $2,000); paid by check on Sheridan Banking Company
Heirship fee authorized by Indian Office; deducted from estate total of $1,521.24
Check No. 10613 representing Emily J. Geisdorff's share (one half plus dower interest) of estate proceeds from sale of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment
Share of estate proceeds credited to minor daughter Emily Lucile Geisdorff
Lease money deposited to credit of estate in Merchants National Bank of Billings, Montana
Government farmer appraised value of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s 159.67-acre allotment
Total amount on books at Crow Agency to credit of Frederick Geisdorff deceased, before deduction of heirship fee
Ten percent deposit submitted with bid for Crow Land Sale No. 991
Balance of purchase price paid by check on Sheridan Banking Company for Crow Land Sale No. 991

relationships.context: Allottee was living at Randlett, Utah under jurisdiction of Uintah and Ouray School and Superintendent Kneale

relationships.context: Acting Assistant Commissioner; cleared land for fee patent issuance October 13, 1919

relationships.context: Crow Indian allottee on Crow Reservation, Montana

correspondence.subject: Leasing papers for allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Answer regarding leasing of allotment

correspondence.subject: Age issue and sale approval doubts
correspondence.action_requested: Submit date of birth and names of relatives; obtain statement from Supt. Kneale recommending approval
correspondence.outcome: No reply received from allottee before his death

correspondence.subject: Receipt of payment checks for Crow Land Sale No. 991
correspondence.action_requested: Acknowledgment of receipt of two checks for $1,350 and $150
correspondence.outcome: Checks held pending Indian Bureau approval

correspondence.subject: Death of Frederick Geisdorff Jr.
correspondence.action_requested: None; informational
correspondence.outcome: Crow Agency informed of allottee's death from influenza

correspondence.subject: Age issue of allottee; possible cancellation of sale
correspondence.action_requested: Powers may request return of payment if sale cancelled
correspondence.outcome: Sale not immediately cancelled; awaiting further information

correspondence.subject: Request for assistance paying note owed by deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Financial assistance

correspondence.subject: Response to request for financial assistance; status of pending sale
correspondence.action_requested: Informed widow of pending sale uncertainty and upcoming heirship hearing
correspondence.outcome: No funds provided; hearing to be advertised

correspondence.subject: Review of all matters concerning land sale of Frederick Geisdorff Jr. allotment
correspondence.action_requested: Instructions on how to proceed with sale; holding checks from Powers for $1,350 and $150

correspondence.subject: Pending sale of allotment; inquiry whether widow wishes sale completed
correspondence.action_requested: Widow to indicate whether she wants pending sale completed

correspondence.sender: Chief Clerk, Office of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Response to March 24, 1919 letter regarding Frederick Geisdorff Jr. land sale
correspondence.action_requested: If probable heirs want sale to proceed, prepare and forward sale papers; otherwise reject bid and readvertise
correspondence.outcome: Sale papers eventually prepared and forwarded

correspondence.subject: Proposed sale of allotment of deceased husband
correspondence.action_requested: Sign enclosed authority if she wishes sale to proceed to Powers for $1,500; write if she does not

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991; enclosing sale papers
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale
correspondence.outcome: Sale approved; fee patent issued November 14, 1919

correspondence.subject: Completion of Land Sale No. 991; request for approval
correspondence.action_requested: Approval of sale at early date; Powers requesting return of deposit if no definite action

correspondence.subject: Copy of letter to Indian Bureau regarding Geisdorff sale
correspondence.action_requested: Informational

correspondence.subject: Transmittal of fee patent No. 719146 to Thomas R. Powers
correspondence.action_requested: Make proper notations on records; deliver patent to Powers upon signing receipt in duplicate; forward original receipt to Washington

correspondence.subject: Proceeds of sale; minor's share
correspondence.action_requested: Informed widow that proceeds can be transferred to her agency after heirs determined; minor's shares require special authority

correspondence.subject: Fee patent No. 719146 issued to Thomas R. Powers on allotment No. 2336
correspondence.action_requested: Notification of patent issuance

correspondence.action_requested: Consideration of heirship report and recommendations

correspondence.outcome: Payment distributed to widow and daughter

correspondence.subject: Inquiry about proceeds of sale and proposed investment in lands
correspondence.action_requested: Transfer of proceeds and information about investment

legislative_actions.outcome: Dawes Act; basis for trust patent issued December 2, 1907 for Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment
Act under which Frederick Geisdorff Jr.'s allotment was made as amended
Act under which Francis Geisdorff's homestead allotment of 648.04 acres was made; trust patent No. 227584 issued December 21, 1923
Referenced in fee patent approval for Thomas R. Powers regarding water rights and reclamation provisions
Referenced in 1979 analysis as basis for Crow Tribe's ownership claim to allotted lands upon allottee's death
